info.title: Comply Lens Backend - Compliance Platform
info.description:
## Comply Lens - Regulatory & OSS Compliance Platform
    
    Comprehensive compliance platform supporting both regulatory (GDPR, DORA) and OSS/Copyright compliance.
    
    ### Regulatory Compliance Features
    
    * **Document Management**: Upload and process regulation and policy documents
    * **Gap Analysis**: AI-powered semantic analysis to identify compliance gaps
    * **Temporal Tracking**: Track gaps over time with status updates (NEW, EXISTING, RESOLVED, WORSENED)
    * **Anomaly Detection**: Detect unusual spikes in gaps or risk scores
    * **Policy Comparison**: Compare document versions and track changes
    * **Remediation Roadmap**: AI-powered remediation strategies with effort estimation
    * **Interactive Compliance Assistant**: ChatOps interface for compliance queries
    
    ### OSS & Copyright Compliance Features (NEW)
    
    * **CVE Vulnerability Scanning**: Real-time vulnerability checks using NIST NVD API
    * **License Compatibility**: Automated license conflict detection (20+ licenses supported)
    * **Comprehensive Reports**: Full compliance reports with security and legal analysis
    * **Batch Processing**: Check multiple packages in a single request
    * **Risk Assessment**: Critical, High, Medium, Low risk classification
    
    ### Infrastructure
    
    * **Caching**: Redis-based caching for fast report retrieval
    * **Rate Limiting**: Distributed rate limiting for API protection
    * **Async Processing**: Background task processing for long-running operations
    
    ### AI Providers
    
    Supports multiple AI providers through adapter pattern:
    - Google Gemini
    - OpenAI GPT
    - Anthropic Claude
    
    Configure via `AI_PROVIDER` environment variable.
    
    ### Authentication
    
    This API uses OAuth2 with Keycloak for authentication. Click "Authorize" button to log in.
    
    **Organization Context:**
    - Organizations are automatically selected based on your user profile
    - Your JWT token will include your organization_id claim
    - Users created via seed script:
      - alice@acme.com / alice123 (Acme Corp - organization admin)
      - bob@acme.com / bob123 (Acme Corp - compliance viewer)
      - charlie@techstart.com / charlie123 (TechStart Inc - organization admin)
      - diana@techstart.com / diana123 (TechStart Inc - compliance viewer)
    
    ### Workflow
    
    1. Upload regulation document (`POST /documents/upload`)
    2. Upload policy document (`POST /documents/upload`)
    3. Wait for processing to complete (check status via `GET /documents/{id}`)
    4. Run gap analysis (`POST /analysis/run`)
    5. Retrieve analysis report (`GET /analysis/{report_id}`)

post.description:
Login with username and password (Resource Owner Password Credentials flow).

This endpoint exchanges username/password for a JWT token from Keycloak.

**Test Users:**
- username: john.doe@acme-corp.com, password: password123
- username: jane.smith@acme-corp.com, password: password123
- username: alice.johnson@techstart.io, password: password123
- username: bob.wilson@techstart.io, password: password123

**Response includes:**
- access_token: JWT token to use in Authorization header
- expires_in: Token expiration time in seconds
- organization_id: User's organization (if present in token)

Refresh an expired access token using a refresh token.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "refresh_token": "your-refresh-token"
}
```

Upload a regulation or policy document for processing.

- Accepts PDF and TXT files
- Processing happens asynchronously in background
- Returns document metadata immediately
- Multi-tenant: Scoped to organization specified in JWT token

get.description:
Retrieve a document by its ID.

Returns document metadata including processing status.
Multi-tenant: Only returns documents belonging to the specified organization.

get.summary: Get extracted text content of document
get.description:
Get the extracted text content of a document.

Returns the processed/extracted text content of the document.
Only available after document has been processed (status == 'processed').
Multi-tenant: Only returns content for documents belonging to the organization.

schema.title: Response Get Document Content Api Documents  Doc Id  Content Get

get.description:
List all uploaded documents.

- Returns up to `limit` documents
- Ordered by creation date (newest first)
- Multi-tenant: Only returns documents belonging to the specified organization

schema.title: Response List Documents Api Documents  Get

post.summary: Upload new version of existing document
post.description:
Upload a new version of an existing document.

- Creates a new document linked to the original via parent_document_id
- Automatically increments version number
- Marks previous versions as not latest
- Document type inherited from parent
- Multi-tenant: Parent document must belong to the specified organization

Use this to track policy/regulation updates over time while maintaining
a complete version history.

get.description:
Get complete version history for a document family.

Returns all versions of the document, showing:
- Version numbers
- Which is the latest
- How many analyses use each version
- Processing status

Works with any document ID in the family (returns the whole family).
Multi-tenant: Only returns versions belonging to the specified organization.

Compare two document versions and show differences.

Supports multiple output formats:
- **json**: Structured diff data (default)
- **html**: HTML formatted diff for web display
- **markdown**: Markdown formatted diff for documentation
- **unified**: Git-style unified diff format

The comparison shows:
- Additions (new text in newer version)
- Deletions (text removed from older version)
- Modifications (text changed between versions)
- Overall similarity percentage

Multi-tenant: Both documents must belong to the specified organization.

post.description:
Search documents using semantic similarity (RAG-powered).
    
    This endpoint uses vector embeddings to find relevant document sections
    based on semantic meaning, not just keyword matching.
    
    **Example queries**:
    - "What are the data retention requirements?"
    - "Encryption standards for personal data"
    - "User consent and withdrawal procedures"
    
    Multi-tenant: Searches only within documents belonging to the specified organization.

Create a new conversation.

- Multi-tenant: Scoped to organization specified in JWT token

get.description:
List conversations for current organization.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns conversations for the specified organization
- Optional user filter to see specific user's conversations
- Supports pagination via limit/offset

Get detailed conversation information.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns if conversation belongs to organization
- Returns 404 if not found or belongs to different organization
- Optionally includes message history

patch.description:
Update conversation details.

- Multi-tenant: Conversation must belong to organization
- Can update title and status

delete.description:
Archive a conversation (soft delete).

- Multi-tenant: Conversation must belong to organization
- Sets status to ARCHIVED
- Preserves all data for audit trail

post.description:
Send a message in a conversation.

- Multi-tenant: Conversation must belong to organization
- Creates message with automatic sequence numbering
- Updates conversation activity timestamp

get.description:
Get messages for a conversation.

- Multi-tenant: Conversation must belong to organization
- Returns messages in chronological order
- Supports pagination

post.description:
Add contextual entity to a conversation.

- Multi-tenant: Conversation must belong to organization
- Links documents, analyses, or other entities to conversation
- Used for context-aware AI responses

patch.description:
Submit user feedback for a message.

- Multi-tenant: Message must belong to organization
- Used to rate AI responses and collect feedback

get.description:
Get overall statistics for all conversations in organization.

- Multi-tenant: Scoped to current user's organization
- Returns total conversations, active conversations, message counts, etc.

schema.title: Response Get Conversation Statistics Api Chat Statistics Get

get.description:
Get statistics for a conversation.

- Multi-tenant: Conversation must belong to organization
- Returns message counts, token usage, etc.

post.description:
Create a new analysis report.

- Requires JWT authentication
- Multi-tenant: Scoped to user's organization
- Documents must exist and belong to the organization
- Report starts in 'pending' status

get.description:
List analysis reports for current organization.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns reports for the specified organization
- Supports pagination via limit/offset
- Ordered by creation date (newest first)

Get overall statistics for all reports in the organization.

Returns counts and metrics across all analysis reports.

schema.title: Response Get Reports Statistics Api Analysis Reports Statistics Get

get.description:
Get analysis report by ID with its gaps.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns if report belongs to organization
- Returns 404 if not found or belongs to different organization
- Includes all gaps and optionally gap statistics

patch.description:
Update analysis report details.

- Multi-tenant: Report must belong to organization
- Can update status, scores, summary, recommendations

get.description:
List analysis reports that use a specific document.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns reports for the organization
- Document can be either regulation or policy

post.description:
Create a new gap for an analysis report.

- Multi-tenant: Report must belong to organization
- Gap is automatically associated with the organization

get.description:
List gaps for current organization.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns gaps for the specified organization
- Optional status filter
- Supports pagination

Get gap by ID.

- Multi-tenant: Only returns if gap belongs to organization

patch.description:
Update gap details.

- Multi-tenant: Gap must belong to organization
- Can update title, description, severity, recommendation, etc.

Update gap status (with automatic snapshot creation).

- Multi-tenant: Gap must belong to organization
- Automatically creates historical snapshot
- If status is RESOLVED, records resolver and timestamp

get.description:
Get historical snapshots for a gap.

- Multi-tenant: Gap must belong to organization
- Returns snapshots ordered by creation date (newest first)
- Useful for tracking gap evolution over time

Get gap statistics for organization or specific report.

- Multi-tenant: Only includes gaps for the organization
- Optional report_id filter
- Returns counts by status and severity

Get gap severity distribution for a specific report.

Returns count of gaps by severity level (critical, high, medium, low, info).
Multi-tenant: Report must belong to the organization.

schema.title: Response Get Severity Distribution Api Analysis Reports  Report Id  Severity Distribution Get

post.description:
Create a temporal analysis of document changes over time.

Analyzes how compliance gaps have evolved across document versions.
Multi-tenant: Document must belong to the organization.

schema.title: Response Create Temporal Analysis Api Analysis Temporal Analysis Post

get.description:
Get temporal trends of gaps and compliance over time.

Shows how gap identification and compliance status have evolved.
Multi-tenant: Only includes data for organization.

schema.title: Response Get Temporal Trends Api Analysis Temporal Trends Get

get.description:
Export analysis report in various formats.

Supported formats:
- **pdf**: PDF document (default)
- **json**: Structured JSON data
- **csv**: Comma-separated values (for spreadsheets)

Multi-tenant: Report must belong to the organization.

post.description:
Execute gap analysis using GapAnalyzer service.

- Fetches regulation and policy documents
- Runs AI-powered gap analysis
- Creates gap records in database
- Updates report status to 'completed'
- Multi-tenant: Report and documents must belong to organization

get.description:
Count how many analyses use a specific document.

**Service-to-Service Communication Pattern**:
This endpoint enables Document Service to query document usage
without creating a database-level dependency.

- Multi-tenant: Only counts analyses for the specified organization
- Returns count for both regulation and policy document usage

**Usage**: Document Service can call this endpoint via HTTP to get
the analysis count for version history displays.

post.description: Create a new remediation plan.

get.description: List all remediation plans.
Get remediation plan with its steps.
List plans for an analysis report.

post.description: Create a remediation step.

get.description: Get remediation step by ID.

patch.description: Update step status with automatic progress tracking.

post.description: Create a policy template.

get.description: List policy templates.
Get policy template by ID.

post.description: Increment template usage count.
Create a copyright statute.

get.description: List copyright statutes.
Get statute by code.

post.description: Create an OSS license.

get.description: List OSS licenses.
Get license by SPDX ID.

post.description: Create a package vulnerability.

get.description: List all vulnerabilities.
Get vulnerability statistics for organization.
Get vulnerability by ID.
List vulnerabilities for a specific package.
Check package for CVE vulnerabilities using NIST NVD (live).
Get license information for a package (live).

Check license compatibility between project and dependencies (live).

Args:
    project_license: Project's primary license (e.g., MIT, Apache-2.0)
    dependency_licenses: Comma-separated list of dependency licenses to check against

Returns:
    Compatibility check results with risk assessment

post.description: Create an audit log entry.

get.description: List audit logs with filters.
Get audit log by ID.
Get audit statistics for organization.

post.description: Create a chat audit log entry.

get.description: List chat audit logs with filters.
Get chat audit log by ID.

patch.description: Update user feedback for a chat log.

get.description: Get chat audit statistics for organization.

Health check endpoint.

Returns basic API information.

Detailed health check endpoint.

Returns health status of all components.
